title: Agalinis skinneriana
intro: Agalinis skinneriana, also known as Skinner's agalinis, is a perennial plant species within the Orobanchaceae family of flowering plants, commonly found in the eastern United States. Traditionally, Skinner's agalinis has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Skinner's agalinis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in tincture and extract form in traditional herbal medicine.
taxonomy: Agalinis skinneriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is classified as a type of holoparasite, meaning it obtains all of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. As a member of the Agalinis genus, it is closely related to other broomrape species. The classification of Agalinis skinneriana is further supported by its unique characteristics and physical features.
